area_anchor: Brikette — Prime messaging threads show incomplete data and mixed status codes with email threads
current_truth: Prime threads return empty arrays for events and admissionOutcomes. Draft status uses a different enum set than email threads with no type discriminator. Analytics queries exclude Prime threads entirely. The draft lifecycle for Prime is incomplete — no regeneration guard exists.
next_scope_now: Add a source discriminator field to thread/draft types. Map Prime-specific fields (events, admissionOutcomes, status) correctly. Include Prime threads in analytics with source filtering. Add regeneration guard for Prime drafts.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Prime threads missing events/admissions; status enum mismatch; analytics exclusion; no regenerate guard
why: Prime messaging threads show up in the inbox but with missing information and inconsistent status labels compared to email threads. Staff can't see the full picture for Prime conversations, analytics don't count them, and there's no protection against duplicate draft generation. This creates a fragmented experience where email and Prime threads behave differently.

intended_outcome.statement: Prime and email threads use a shared type system with source discrimination, analytics include both sources with filtering, and Prime drafts have regeneration protection.
